title: Observation of interaction-induced fast Thouless pumping of solitons
authors: Yuqing Li, Jinxiong Jia, Yunfei Wang, Huiying Du, Zhong An, Zhenhua Qiao, Liantuan Xiao, Suotang Jia, Qian Niu, Jie Ma
abstract: Thouless pumping provides a paradigmatic platform for studying the effects of interactions on topological transport in periodically driven systems. However, most studies have been constrained by adiabatic conditions, which preclude exploration of interaction-driven novel topological states at high driving frequencies. Here, we experimentally investigate the interplay between interaction and modulation frequency in Thouless pumping realized in a periodically modulated lattice in momentum space of atomic Bose-Einstein condensate. We observe fast Thouless pumping of matterwave solitons at intermediate interactions, with no counterpart in the non- or weakly interacting regimes. Beyond the boundary of topological phase transition induced by interaction, nonadiabatic quantized pumping of solitons emerges at high modulation frequencies over a broad interaction range, in good agreement with theoretical calculations, while the solitons remain trapped in the low-frequency adiabatic pumping regime. Our work opens new avenues for accelerating topological transport in driven quantum systems and engineering fast topological devices.
summary: This paper experimentally investigates Thouless pumping in an atomic Bose-Einstein condensate, demonstrating that interactions can induce fast, non-adiabatic topological transport of matter-wave solitons. By modulating the lattice and tuning interactions, the researchers achieved quantized pumping regimes inaccessible under purely adiabatic conditions. This opens new avenues for engineering fast topological quantum devices using ultracold atoms.
main_problem: The study investigates how interparticle interactions influence topological transport, specifically Thouless pumping, in periodically driven quantum systems, aiming to operate beyond the limitations of adiabatic driving.
main_result: The authors observed fast, interaction-induced Thouless pumping of matter-wave solitons at intermediate interaction strengths, and demonstrated nonadiabatic quantized pumping at high modulation frequencies.
method: The research uses experimental realization in an atomic Bose-Einstein condensate, measuring the center-of-mass displacement and participation ratio to diagnose topological transport.
model_or_system: The physical system is an atomic Bose-Einstein condensate (BEC) of ${}^{133}	ext{Cs}$ atoms trapped in a periodically modulated lattice in momentum space. The dynamics are modeled by a nonlinear Schrödinger equation incorporating both lattice hopping and mean-field interaction terms.
key_observables: Center-of-mass displacement ($\delta D$), participation ratio ($r$), and the Chern number (quantized pumping value).
important_parameters: Interaction strength ($U$), modulation frequency ($\Omega$), and modulation amplitude ($\Delta J/J_m$).
main_assumptions: The analysis relies on the mean-field approximation, and initial state preparation is noted as an approximation.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the experimental setup, the dependence of the Chern number on interaction, and measurements of $\delta D$ and $r$ over pump cycles for varying interaction and modulation parameters.
paper_structure: The paper progresses by introducing the physical platform and model, presenting experimental measurements of key observables ($\delta D, r$) across different interaction regimes, and finally developing the theoretical understanding of the nonadiabatic pumping mechanism.
why_interesting: This work directly bridges concepts from topological physics (Thouless pumping) with experimental quantum simulation using ultracold atoms, providing a clean platform to study nonlinear, interaction-driven quantum phenomena.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

Realization of Thouless pumping with tunable in- teraction. a, Sketch of experimental setup for coupling dis- crete momentum states of condensed 133Cs atoms via multiple counter-propagating Bragg laser pairs, whose frequency dif- ferences are controlled by engineering multi-frequency compo- nents ωn. b, Atomic interaction is adjusted by tuning the s- wave scattering length a, which varies with the magnetic field B through a broad Feshbach resonance. c, Atomic discrete momentum states pn = 2nℏk (n = −9, · · · , 11) are coupled by the Bragg laser pairs to realize an off-diagonal Aubry-Andr´e- Harper model with three sites (A, B, C) per unit cell. d, Peri- odic modulation of coupling...

FIG. 2. Interaction-driven fast quantized Thouless pumping. a-d, Measured atomic center-of-mass displacement δD (orange circles) and participation ratio r (green circles) as a function of evolution time Ωt for different interactions U/Jm. e,f, Numeri- cally calculated instantaneous energy bands for U/Jm = 1.5 and 2 under the mean-field approximation and periodic boundary condition. Color scale represents the projection of time-evolved state |ϕ(t)⟩onto the instantaneous eigenstates |ψNL(t)⟩. g,h, Variations of δD and r measured after a pump cycle Ωt = 2π with U/Jm. The solid lines in a-d, g and h are numerical simulations based on Eq. (1). The error bars denote standard deviations. The...

FIG. 3. Interaction-induced nonadiabatic quantized Thouless pumping. a,b, Phase diagrams of Thouless pumping under the interplay between interaction U/Jm and modulation frequency Ω/Jm. Measured (a) and calculated (b) atomic center-of-mass displacement δD obtained after a pump cycle as a function of U/Jm and Ω/Jm, where the hatched regime in a denotes the experimentally inaccessible low-frequency region due to the limitation of decoherence on the long-time propagation. Star points in b mark the parameters of (U/Jm, Ω/Jm) = (2, 0.1), (2.2, 0.1) and (2.2, 0.5), respectively. Nonadiabatic quantized Thouless pumping emerges at the blue region beyond the phase boundary (dashed line). c-e, The...

FIG. 4. Dependence of Thouless pumping on the modulation amplitude. a-c, The atomic center-of-mass displacement δD measured after a pump cycle Ωt = 2π as a function of modulation amplitude ∆J/Jm for different interactions U/Jm. The solid lines are numerical simulations based on Eq. (1). The error bars denote standard deviations. The modulation frequency is Ω/Jm = 0.5 with Jm/ℏ= 2π × 1kHz.
